fetch_status: WebSearch fallback yielded major HN front-page stories from May 2026: Penn exciton-polariton, JUPITER 50-qubit, Aalto quantum-inspired algorithm, Jack Clark intelligence explosion prediction, METR 12hr autonomy, SWE-Bench 93.9%, OpenAI Daybreak, Microsoft MDASH.

atoms.text: Penn researchers created exciton-polaritons (hybrid light-matter quasiparticles) for all-light switching using ~4 femtojoules per operation, removing the photon-to-electron conversion bottleneck in photonic AI computing.

If exciton-polariton photonic chips scale, AI compute moves to a substrate where matter and light are entangled at the device level; the energy-per-operation floor drops by orders of magnitude vs CMOS.

JUPITER (Europe's first exascale supercomputer) simulated 50-qubit universal quantum computer for the first time; classical simulation of quantum systems is tracking quantum hardware capacity at exascale.

Aalto researchers introduced a quantum-inspired algorithm that handles massive non-periodic systems (e.g., >1e15 numbers for quasicrystals) on classical hardware; the boundary between classical and quantum tractability is moving via algorithmic, not hardware, advances.

atoms.post_title: Tensor networks crack supposed quantum-only problem
atoms.text: Flatiron Institute CCQ team used tensor-network compression to solve a quantum-physics problem previously claimed to be quantum-computer-only; mathematical compression of wave functions defeats quantum supremacy in this domain.

Jack Clark (Anthropic co-founder): 60%+ chance by end of 2028 that an AI system can be asked 'make a better version of yourself' and autonomously do so. Intelligence explosion exits AI safety theory and becomes timeline forecast.
METR data: AI systems' autonomous task-duration grew from 30 seconds (2022) to ~12 hours (2026). The autonomy curve is itself a measurable variable now, with empirical doubling time.

Top AI models on SWE-Bench: 2% (2023) -> 33% (Aug 2024) -> 81% (Dec 2025) -> 93.9% (2026). Real-world coding-task success scaled superlinearly; the curve crosses human-baseline well before the model curve flattens.

OpenAI launched Daybreak with GPT-5.5-Cyber for AI-powered vulnerability detection + patch validation; cybersec AI now has a deployment-grade tool, mirroring Anthropic Mythos but on the defender side.

atoms.post_title: Microsoft MDASH multi-model AI cybersecurity system
atoms.text: Microsoft's MDASH uses model-agnostic bespoke AI agents per vulnerability class; cybersec architecture is fragmenting into per-class agent specialists, not monolithic models.

atoms.post_title: Karpathy joins Anthropic for pre-training research
atoms.text: Karpathy moves to Anthropic specifically to accelerate pre-training research using Claude itself — meta-level: the post-training stack is being designed using the current post-training stack. Recursive RSI infrastructure step.

First known AI-developed 2FA-bypass zero-day for mass exploitation appears (May 2026). The offensive-side capability curve crosses an economically meaningful threshold; AI-assisted attacks are now a mass-market phenomenon, not lab demo.
